methods.description: Process for systematically collecting and preparing sediment and shell samples for radiocarbon dating and analysis.

steps.description: Samples were taken systematically with an interval of 10-15 cm in the fine units and of 20 cm in the thick units.
steps.searchable_summary: collect samples at specified intervals from sediment profiles

steps.description: The dates used in this study are based on a radiocarbon analysis (absolute dating using 14C), conducted in A.E. Lalonde AMS Laboratory using an accelerator mass spectrometer.
steps.searchable_summary: perform radiocarbon dating using accelerator mass spectrometer

steps.description: The absolute dating was undertaken on broken shells (diameter less than 2 mm) from the shell-rich levels along the profile the most representative of the levels encountered.
steps.searchable_summary: select and date broken shells from shell-rich levels

methods.description: Procedures for analyzing sediment samples using laser-particle measurement and mineralogical identification.

steps.description: The sedimentological analysis was performed using a laser-particle-measurement instrument (Coulter LS1330 at the University of Perpignan).
steps.searchable_summary: analyze sediment samples using laser-particle measurement

steps.description: The mineralogical identification was firstly made with a binocular magnifying glass, then by a diffractometer with the X-ray Bruker D8 Advance at the University of Biskra.
steps.searchable_summary: identify minerals using binocular magnifying glass and X-ray diffractometer

steps.description: The spectra was treated by the Much 2.0 software.

methods.description: Procedure for identifying molluscan species from collected samples using standard protocols.

steps.description: Molluscan identification was undertaken following standard protocols (Evans, 1972; Limondin-Lozouet, 2002b; Lozek, 1964; Puisségur, 1976; Rousseau, 1986, 1987).
steps.searchable_summary: identify molluscan species using standard protocols

steps.description: The large shells were harvested manually, and all species of the adult size class, ranging from 0.5 to 25 mm, were extracted or sieved from the fine fraction with a 2 mm sieve.
steps.searchable_summary: extract and sieve molluscan species from samples

steps.description: Smaller species and broken shells, invisible to the naked eye or existing as debris, which could not be identified, were excluded from the study.
steps.searchable_summary: exclude unidentifiable small species and debris
